summary: One machine owns the Chroma index; no sync between hosts
rationale: Concurrent writes from two hosts corrupt the HNSW index silently. rsync between dev and miniPC was tried and rejected because it creates a dual-writer problem where refine/monitor writes on the miniPC diverge from index writes on the dev machine.
alternatives_rejected: rsync Chroma between dev and miniPC
NFS mount from miniPC to dev
constraints: Only one host writes to ~/.local/share/convmem/chroma/ at a time
MiniPC services disabled when dev machine is canonical

summary: Run watch/refine/monitor on dev machine, not miniPC
rationale: Transcripts are created by Cursor/Kiro/Aider on the dev machine. Syncing them to the miniPC adds fragile rsync complexity for zero retrieval benefit. The miniPC lacks the source files and GPU for full ingest.
alternatives_rejected: miniPC indexes transcripts via rsync from dev
miniPC runs all services with remote Ollama on dev machine
constraints: Re-enable miniPC services only if dev machine becomes unavailable long-term

summary: Queue semantic duplicate candidates for Kiro review; never auto-merge in v1
rationale: Auto-merge is irreversible. False positives (units that look similar but represent different contexts) delete real knowledge. At current corpus scale (~1700 units) the dedupe queue is manageable for human review.
constraints: queue_max_depth pauses semantic_dedupe when queue is full

summary: Monitor skips write if any existing verification has author_model or verifier_model = kiro-review
rationale: Automated low-confidence (0.4) checks must never overwrite a human-signed finding. Monitor pass is advisory only; Kiro confirmation required before treating as resolved.
alternatives_rejected: Monitor emits verification regardless of prior authority
constraints: Check both author_model and verifier_model fields
Log skip with reason when Kiro verification exists

summary: Append rationale to Chroma document text so LLM sees it in ask context
rationale: Metadata-only storage is invisible to the retrieval and generation path. The ask prompt builds context from the document field only. Without rationale in the document, convmem ask 'why X' cannot answer even when the decision record exists in the index.
alternatives_rejected: Add a separate metadata-reading step in ask.py
constraints: Document text format: summary + keywords + ' Rationale: ' + rationale
Rationale is optional — decisions without it still ingest cleanly
